mappings.rationaleAI: The Hippocratic Oath covers the foundational ethical code for medical practice including prohibitions on harm, patient confidentiality, and contemporary debates about euthanasia and genetic engineering -- directly relevant to the ethical basis of medical practice and medical education.

Man and Disease covers the development of epidemiology, public health infrastructure, vaccination, and the relationship between urbanisation and disease -- relevant to medical practice, public health services, and prevention of infection.

Immunisation covers vaccination and public health campaigns from Jenner through modern vaccine development and anti-vaccination movements -- directly relevant to vaccination/immunization and prevention of epidemics.

Louis Pasteur's germ theory, pasteurisation, and vaccine development transformed medical practice and public health -- directly relevant to destruction of infectious agents, prophylactic medication, and public health.

Microbiology covers germ theory, Koch's postulates, antibiotics, vaccines, and the microbiome -- directly relevant to specialized medical research, public health, and destruction of infectious agents.
The Great Stink covers London's sanitation crisis, cholera epidemics, and Bazalgette's sewer system -- directly relevant to public health services and environmental sanitation.

Anaesthetics covers the development of anaesthesiology as a medical specialty and the transformation of surgical practice -- directly relevant to specialized medical fields and hospital/specialist practice.
Anatomy covers Vesalius's anatomical revolution, anatomical theatres, body snatching, and the Anatomy Act of 1832 -- directly relevant to medical education and specialized medical research.

Drugs covers the regulation of pharmacologically active substances, drug control treaties, and the pharmaceutical industry -- directly relevant to health and safety laws controlling foods and drugs, and prophylactic medication.
Avicenna's Canon of Medicine dominated medical education for centuries, representing a foundational text for understanding the historical practice of medicine and specialist medical knowledge.
The Oath episode covers the Hippocratic Oath in its classical context as a binding religious and professional instrument -- directly relevant to the ethical basis of medical practice.

Animal Experiments and Rights covers the ethics of vivisection and animal models in biomedical research, the three Rs framework, and regulatory frameworks -- relevant to the ethical basis of medical practice and specialized medical research.
Penicillin's discovery and development as the first antibiotic, and the problem of antibiotic resistance, is directly relevant to the use of therapeutic agents and prophylactic medication.
Echolocation has applications in medical imaging technology (ultrasound), tangentially relevant to industrial medicine, but the overlap is too tangential.
